capabilities.name: Context management
capabilities.summary: The repo ships a dedicated context manager and compaction logic, but the primary scan/drain execution path does not route prompt assembly through it today.
capabilities.recommendation: Thread ctxmgr compaction and window selection into prompt construction so long-lived runs degrade gracefully instead of relying on raw prompt truncation alone.

code_evidence.summary: Compaction exists as a standalone helper instead of being called from the runner's prompt path.

capabilities.name: Tool catalog and permissions
capabilities.summary: A rich tool catalog and role-permission model exists, but workflow execution still relies on per-phase strings and provider flags rather than the catalog as the source of truth.
capabilities.recommendation: Route phase/tool authorization through the catalog so allowed tool sets and permission scopes become auditable, reusable runtime policy.

code_evidence.summary: Catalog models tools and role permissions, but the main CLI path does not wire it into phase execution.

capabilities.name: Typed memory
capabilities.summary: Typed memory, scratchpads, and a session KV store are implemented, but vessel execution does not currently persist or hydrate these artifacts across phases.
capabilities.recommendation: Persist mission/vessel memory alongside phase outputs and feed selected memory back into later phases and recurring review workflows.

code_evidence.summary: KVStore and typed memory types exist as reusable primitives without primary-path wiring from scan/drain.

capabilities.name: Workflow orchestration
capabilities.summary: Phase dependency graphs are turned into orchestrator topologies and executed in waves, so orchestration is active in the primary runner path.
capabilities.recommendation: Keep orchestration simple-first and extend only where dependency-based parallelism proves insufficient.

code_evidence.summary: The runner builds orchestrator topology from workflow phases when dependency execution is needed.
The orchestrator package defines the topology and execution patterns used by the runner.

capabilities.name: Verification and evidence
capabilities.summary: The runner persists evidence manifests and the repo includes DTU-backed verification flows, so verification is materially wired into execution artifacts.
capabilities.recommendation: Expand deterministic check coverage before adding more prompt-driven verification logic.

code_evidence.summary: persistRunArtifacts writes evidence manifests and summary artifacts for vessel runs.

capabilities.name: Generator-evaluator loops
capabilities.summary: An evaluator loop abstraction exists, but it is not yet invoked by the main CLI workflows or recurring quality-review flows.
capabilities.recommendation: Use evaluator loops for high-risk workflow phases and recurring self-audits instead of relying on one-shot prompts alone.

code_evidence.summary: NewLoop provides evaluator wiring, but the main runner path does not call it.

capabilities.name: Observability
capabilities.summary: Drain initializes tracing from config and the observability package provides a live tracer, so observability is active in the control plane.
capabilities.recommendation: Keep expanding span coverage around recurring background vessels so weekly self-analysis has first-class telemetry.

code_evidence.summary: Drain enables tracing from config and constructs an observability tracer for runs.
NewTracer initializes the OpenTelemetry tracer used by the CLI.

capabilities.name: Policy and protected surfaces
capabilities.summary: Protected-surface policy is enforced through the intermediary and attached to runner execution, so baseline security controls are active today.
capabilities.recommendation: Continue tightening policy around externally visible actions while keeping worktree-local execution ergonomic.

code_evidence.summary: DefaultPolicy denies writes to protected xylem control surfaces while allowing other actions.
Drain wires intermediary policy enforcement into runner execution.

capabilities.name: Entropy management
capabilities.summary: Signal heuristics for repetition and stalls exist, but there is no recurring garbage-collection or simplification loop wired into weekly operations yet.
capabilities.recommendation: Use signal heuristics to trigger recurring cleanup and self-gap-analysis actions instead of leaving entropy detection as a library-only primitive.

code_evidence.summary: Signal configuration tracks repetition and stall windows, but the daemon does not yet schedule cleanup based on those signals.
Stall detection exists as a reusable heuristic rather than an active weekly control loop.

capabilities.name: Cost controls
capabilities.summary: Cost tracking, budget alerts, and budget-exceeded short-circuiting are active in vessel execution and summaries.
capabilities.recommendation: Extend cost reporting to recurring self-analysis output so convergence work stays budget-aware over time.

code_evidence.summary: Tracker accumulates usage and enforces budget constraints.
The runner fails vessels when the active cost tracker reports budget exhaustion.
